Now that you know how to create a component, import it, and render it, let’s learn one of the most powerful concepts in React:
Component Nesting.
Imagine you’re building a house.
You don’t pour all the cement into one giant block and call it a house.
Instead, you build it room by room.

• Living Room

• Kitchen

• Bedroom

• Bathroom

• Balcony
Each room is independent.
When all the rooms come together, they form a beautiful home.
React applications are built exactly the same way.
Instead of creating one huge component, we build many smaller components and combine them together.
This process is called Component Nesting.
Understanding Component Nesting
Suppose you’re creating a portfolio website.
Instead of writing everything inside App.jsx, you split it into reusable components.
App
│
├── Navbar
├── Hero
├── About
├──…
